title: Goofin' in the Gallatin
summary: The first stop when heading into the Gallatin Valley was Bozeman Hotsprings. We hadn't showered since we left Missoula so we decided to stop here to relax and get clean. After our soak, we headed up Stormcastle Road in Gallatin Canyon to find a camp spot. We found a little pull off next to a little creek to post up for the night. The next morning, we did some underwhelming climbing at the crag down the road. We were rather unenthused about the climbing, so we bailed a little early to go on a run on the trails nearby. After the run, we found another camp spot along the road, had an early dinner and got a good nights sleep. Before leaving the canyon, I went on a run up Stormcastle Peak, which had great views of the Gallatin Mountains. On our way through Bozeman, we stopped at Helen's uncle's house as it happened to be her cousin's graduation party that day. We enjoyed a few hours of family time before stocking up groceries at WinCo (our favorite grocery store) and hitting the road.

photos.caption: Helens (very) belated birthday dinner at Whistle Pig in Bozeman.
